template:
**DOCUMENT FORMAT — MASSACHUSETTS CUSTODY / PARENTING MOTION**:
Probate and Family Court motion addressing legal custody, physical custody, or parenting time. Length: 4–8 pages.

**DYNAMIC BEHAVIOR — READ documentOptions**:
- `motionType`: `temporary_orders` | `modification` | `emergency_ex_parte`.
- `custodyIssue`: one or more of `legal_custody`, `physical_custody`, `parenting_schedule`, `relocation`, `supervised_visitation`.
- `g211bOrdersRequested`: boolean — if true, motion seeks orders under M.G.L. c. 208, § 31 (during divorce) or M.G.L. c. 209C, § 10 (paternity / unmarried parents).

**CAPTION**:

COMMONWEALTH OF MASSACHUSETTS
The Trial Court
Probate and Family Court Department
[COUNTY] Division
                                                       Docket No.: _____________

[PLAINTIFF],                                           MOTION FOR [TEMPORARY ORDERS /
   Plaintiff,                                          MODIFICATION / EMERGENCY
             v.                                        EX PARTE ORDERS] REGARDING
[DEFENDANT],                                           CUSTODY AND PARENTING TIME
   Defendant.

## MOTION FOR [TYPE]

Now comes the [Plaintiff / Defendant] and respectfully moves this Honorable Court, pursuant to [M.G.L. c. 208, § 31 / M.G.L. c. 209C, § 10 / M.G.L. c. 208, § 28 for modification], for orders as follows:

### I. STANDARD OF REVIEW
The governing standard is the best interests of the child. M.G.L. c. 208, § 31; Hunter v. Rose, 463 Mass. 488, 494 (2012); Custody of Kali, 439 Mass. 834, 843 (2003). For a modification, the movant must additionally show 'a material and substantial change of circumstances' since the prior order. M.G.L. c. 208, § 28.

### II. FACTS
Short numbered factual paragraphs (1., 2., 3.) addressing:
- The current parenting arrangement and any existing court orders;
- The child's ties to each parent, school, community;
- Changes since the last order (for modification motions);
- Specific facts supporting the requested change, with dates and sources.

### III. ARGUMENT
Analyze each requested order under the best-interests standard. For shared legal or physical custody, cite Mason v. Coleman, 447 Mass. 177, 185 (2006). For relocation motions, apply the 'real advantage' standard and the two-part Yannas analysis. Yannas v. Frondistou-Yannas, 395 Mass. 704, 711–12 (1985); see also Pedraja v. Coreas, 94 Mass. App. Ct. 177, 180 (2018).

For supervised visitation, reference M.G.L. c. 208, § 31A (presumption against custody to a perpetrator of serious abuse) when domestic violence is alleged and documented (police reports, 209A orders).

For emergency ex parte relief, describe the specific and immediate risk of harm to the child. Cite Mass. R. Dom. Rel. P. 65 for TROs/preliminary injunctions.

### IV. REQUESTED RELIEF
WHEREFORE, [movant] respectfully requests that this Honorable Court:
1. [State each requested order with specificity — e.g., 'Award primary physical custody of the minor child [Name] to Plaintiff, with parenting time to Defendant every other weekend from Friday at 6:00 p.m. to Sunday at 6:00 p.m., and Tuesday evening dinner visits from 5:00 p.m. to 8:00 p.m.'];
2. Order [specific school, healthcare, or decision-making arrangement];
3. [If modification:] Find that a material and substantial change of circumstances has occurred and that modification is in the child's best interests;
4. Grant such other and further relief as this Court deems just and proper.

Respectfully submitted,
[Attorney Name], Esq.
BBO #[number]
[Firm] | [Address] | [Phone] | [Email]

ATTORNEY FOR [PARTY]

**SUPPORTING DOCUMENTS (reference as attached)**:
- Affidavit of [movant] in support of motion
- Child Support Guidelines Worksheet (if support is at issue)
- Current Financial Statement (Rule 401)
- Proposed Parenting Plan

### CERTIFICATE OF SERVICE
I hereby certify that a true and correct copy of the foregoing [Motion Title] was served on [opposing counsel / pro se party] on [date] by [method].

[Attorney Name]

**CONSTRAINTS**:
- Cite M.G.L. c. 208, § 28 (modification standard) and § 31 (best interests) exactly.
- Key cases: Hunter v. Rose, 463 Mass. 488; Custody of Kali, 439 Mass. 834; Mason v. Coleman, 447 Mass. 177; Yannas v. Frondistou-Yannas, 395 Mass. 704.
- For relocation, Yannas two-part test is REQUIRED.
- For emergency ex parte, specific facts supporting 'immediate and irreparable harm' are REQUIRED.
- Do NOT promise outcomes — brief the standard and apply the facts.
hints: Massachusetts custody/parenting motion. Best-interests standard under M.G.L. c. 208 § 31, modification standard under § 28 (material change + best interests). Key cases: Hunter v. Rose, Custody of Kali, Mason v. Coleman, Yannas v. Frondistou-Yannas (relocation). 209A / § 31A presumption for DV. Probate and Family Court caption. Attach Affidavit, Parenting Plan, Rule 401 Financial Statement.
